summary: A rectangular three-story fortress-like building with four tall corner towers and a recessed central courtyard/roof area. Exterior walls are horizontal oak planks with regular small glass windows and stonebrick/stone slab banding; massive vertical dark oak (or spruce) log towers stand at each corner with overhanging slab roofs capped in a dark red top. A low stonebrick foundation/edge runs around the base and short stone slab balconies/bars project from each floor on the long facades.

shape.overall_form: rectangular main block with four corner towers and an internal recessed roof/courtyard
shape.symmetry: approximate bilateral symmetry along the long axis (mirrored towers and repeating floor bands)

materials.name: wood
materials.role: primary wall (planks) and tower vertical logs

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: roof edge and slab trim around tower tops and horizontal coping

materials.name: brick
materials.role: possible dark red roof top (nether brick or red carpet used) / roof fill

materials.name: fence
materials.role: parapet/railing around recessed roof (small posts visible)

elements: recessed central roof/courtyard with central raised platform
rebuild_hints: Construct a rectangular three-story main hall using horizontal oak planks for walls with evenly spaced 1x1 glass windows in horizontal rows on each floor.

Place vertical dark logs at each corner forming tall towers that extend ~6-8 blocks above the main roof; top towers have overhanging slab_stone trim and a dark red flat cap with a single glow/light block in the center.
Add stonebrick foundation blocks around the base (single-block thick) with repeating stone slab projections in front forming short steps/blocks.
Run stonebrick or stone slab horizontal bands between each floor wrapping the long sides to create balconies; add short stone pillars projecting outward beneath some bands.
Create a recessed central roof area (open courtyard from top) with a raised central square platform of stone (with glow block) and a low fence parapet around the recessed edge.
uncertainties: Exact wood type: appears as oak planks for horizontal walls and dark oak or spruce logs for towers; could be either.
Top roof dark red material: likely nether_brick or red-colored block (carpet or concrete) — uncertain.
Precise interior layout is unknown; courtyard vs interior open space inferred from top-down recessed roof image.
Exact block counts and spacing (width/depth/height) are approximate based on visible screenshots.
